---
title: "Div Anchors Extension Demo"
filters:
  - div-anchors
format:
  html:
    number-sections: true
    anchor-sections: true
  revealjs: default
---

This document demonstrates the `div-anchors` Quarto extension, which adds
visual URL anchor links to theorem divs for HTML and RevealJS output.

Hover over any of the theorem blocks below to see the anchor link (§) appear.
Click it to copy a direct link to that block.

## Theorems

::: {#thm-pythagorean}

### Pythagorean Theorem

For a right triangle with legs $a$ and $b$ and hypotenuse $c$:
$$a^2 + b^2 = c^2.$$
:::

::: {#thm-intermediate-value}
If $f$ is continuous on $[a, b]$ and $k$ is any value between $f(a)$ and
$f(b)$, then there exists $c \in (a, b)$ such that $f(c) = k$.
:::

## Lemmas and Corollaries

::: {#lem-cauchy-schwarz}
For all vectors $\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}$:
$$|\langle \mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v} \rangle|^2
  \leq \langle \mathbf{u}, \mathbf{u} \rangle
       \cdot \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{v} \rangle.$$
:::

::: {#cor-triangle-inequality}
For all vectors $\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}$:
$$\|\mathbf{u} + \mathbf{v}\| \leq \|\mathbf{u}\| + \|\mathbf{v}\|.$$
:::

## Definitions and Examples

::: {#def-prime}
A natural number $p > 1$ is *prime* if it has no positive divisors other
than $1$ and itself.
:::

::: {#exm-primes}
The first five prime numbers are 2, 3, 5, 7, and 11.
:::

## Cross-References

Theorem @thm-pythagorean is often taught in secondary school.
The result in @lem-cauchy-schwarz implies the @cor-triangle-inequality.
See @def-prime and @exm-primes for an introduction to prime numbers.

## Proof (without anchor, unnumbered)

::: {.proof}
Let $a$ and $b$ be the legs of a right triangle and $c$ the hypotenuse.
Arrange four copies of the triangle around a square with side $c$.
The outer shape is a square with side $a + b$, so
$(a + b)^2 = c^2 + 4 \cdot \tfrac{1}{2}ab$,
which simplifies to $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$.
:::
